content: Just as high school language teaching leverages explicit linguistic reflection exercises to help students internalize writing and critical thinking skills, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured scaffolding of language arts reflection curricula to train more consistent, generalizable self-correction capabilities, rather than relying on ad-hoc prompt-based reflection alone. This frames the original Reflexion autonomous agent framework not just as an AI architecture innovation, but as a formalization of the same reflective learning scaffolding humans use to improve performance.
